title: Crinetics Presents Full Results From Phase 2 Trial of Atumelnant in Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) in Oral Presentation at ENDO 2026
author: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
description: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals announced positive Phase 2 trial results for atumelnant, an oral ACTH receptor antagonist for treating congenital adrenal hyperplasia (CAH). The drug demonstrated sustained androgen reductions while enabling patients to lower glucocorticoid supplementation to physiologically normal levels. Additional Phase 1b/2a data in ACTH-dependent Cushing's syndrome showed rapid cortisol reduction. The company is advancing atumelnant into late-phase clinical development with Phase 3 trials already underway.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company reported successful Phase 2 trial results showing atumelnant achieved primary efficacy endpoints with sustained androgen reductions and enabled significant glucocorticoid dose reduction in CAH patients. The drug was well-tolerated with no serious adverse events, and the company is advancing to late-phase development with Phase 3 trials already enrolling, indicating strong clinical progress and commercial potential.

title: Crinetics Presents Long Term Data at ENDO 2026 Confirming PALSONIFY™ (paltusotine) Provides Durable, Consistent Acromegaly Control
author: Na
description: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals announced long-term clinical data from PATHFNDR-1 and PATHFNDR-2 trials showing that PALSONIFY (paltusotine) maintains effective acromegaly control for up to two years with stable IGF-1 levels, symptom control, and pituitary tumor stabilization. Additional data demonstrated improved biochemical control when PALSONIFY is combined with cabergoline. The drug was well-tolerated with no new safety signals identified.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company presented robust long-term clinical data demonstrating durable efficacy and safety of its lead product PALSONIFY across multiple trials. Data shows consistent disease control across all three key metrics (IGF-1 levels, symptoms, tumor volume) with minimal adverse events and low discontinuation rates, supporting the drug's commercial potential and recent FDA/EMA approvals.

title: Crinetics’ Paradigm-Shifting Portfolio and Pipeline on Display at ENDO 2026 with Six Presentations
author: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals
description: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals announced it will present six abstracts at the Endocrine Society's Annual Meeting (ENDO 2026) in June, including oral presentations featuring long-term efficacy and safety data for PALSONIFY (paltusotine) in acromegaly and Phase 2 results for investigational ACTH receptor antagonist atumelnant in congenital adrenal hyperplasia and Cushing's syndrome. The company also reported Q1 2026 net product revenue of $10.3 million for PALSONIFY with 232 enrollment forms.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is presenting multiple clinical trial abstracts at a major medical conference, demonstrating continued development progress for its lead product PALSONIFY which has already received FDA and EMA approvals. Q1 2026 revenue of $10.3 million and 232 enrollment forms indicate commercial traction. Positive clinical data presentations and expansion of pipeline candidates (atumelnant) suggest strong momentum in the company's endocrine disease focus area.

title: Neutrolis Appoints Caren Deardorf as Chief Business & Strategy Officer
author: Not Specified
description: Neutrolis Inc., a clinical-stage biotech company focused on treating autoimmune and inflammatory diseases, announced the appointment of Caren Deardorf as Chief Business & Strategy Officer. Deardorf brings over 30 years of executive leadership experience in commercialization and product launches, previously serving as Chief Commercial & Strategy Officer at Editas Medicine, Magenta Therapeutics, and Ohana Biosciences. She will lead business development and commercial strategy as Neutrolis advances its NTR-1011 candidate following positive Phase 1a results.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned only as a previous employer of the newly appointed executive. No direct business impact or news related to Editas is discussed in the article.
Mentioned only as a board affiliation of the newly appointed executive. No direct business impact or news related to Crinetics is discussed in the article.

title: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals to Report Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results on February 26, 2026
author: Na
description: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals announced it will report Q4 and full year 2025 financial results on February 26, 2026, with a management conference call at 4:30 p.m. ET. The company also recently initiated a Phase 2/3 pediatric trial for atumelnant in congenital adrenal hyperplasia and announced January 2026 inducement grants.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is advancing its clinical pipeline with initiation of a Phase 2/3 pediatric trial for atumelnant in CAH, has an FDA-approved lead product (PALSONIFY), and maintains a robust pipeline of 10+ programs. The announcement of financial results and ongoing clinical development activities demonstrate active business progress and investor engagement.
